$description: Dark-theme overrides applied under [data-theme="dark"]. Inverts the primitive color ramps so every var(--kp-color-*) reference cascades automatically. Contains: (1) primitive ramp inversions for every color in tokens/primitive/color.json, (2) explicit semantic overrides for the handful of tokens (alert/badge subtle backgrounds) where simple inversion produces a surface too close to the page background. Static prelude (color-scheme: dark; background-color; color) is added by the dark-css custom format in style-dictionary.config.js.

black.$description: the new "black" — primary text on dark

600.$description: blue-600 kept at LIGHT value in dark — preserves "hover darker than rest" semantics for action shades

700.$description: blue-700 kept at LIGHT value in dark — preserves "hover darker than rest" semantics for action shades

800.$description: blue-800 kept at LIGHT value in dark — preserves "hover darker than rest" semantics for action shades

600.$description: red-600 kept at LIGHT value in dark — preserves "hover darker than rest" semantics for action shades

700.$description: red-700 kept at LIGHT value in dark — preserves "hover darker than rest" semantics for action shades

800.$description: red-800 kept at LIGHT value in dark — preserves "hover darker than rest" semantics for action shades

600.$description: green-600 kept at LIGHT value in dark — preserves "hover darker than rest" semantics for action shades

700.$description: green-700 kept at LIGHT value in dark — preserves "hover darker than rest" semantics for action shades

800.$description: green-800 kept at LIGHT value in dark — preserves "hover darker than rest" semantics for action shades

600.$description: amber-600 kept at LIGHT value in dark — preserves "hover darker than rest" semantics for action shades

700.$description: amber-700 kept at LIGHT value in dark — preserves "hover darker than rest" semantics for action shades

800.$description: amber-800 kept at LIGHT value in dark — preserves "hover darker than rest" semantics for action shades

600.$description: cyan-600 kept at LIGHT value in dark — preserves "hover darker than rest" semantics for action shades

700.$description: cyan-700 kept at LIGHT value in dark — preserves "hover darker than rest" semantics for action shades

800.$description: cyan-800 kept at LIGHT value in dark — preserves "hover darker than rest" semantics for action shades

badge.$description: Subtle backgrounds — the inverted primitive ramp lands too close to page bg (#09090B). Bump one step brighter so subtle badges read as tinted surfaces. Subtle fg's were picked by the designer-pass on 2026-05-01 against axe-flagged contrast violations.

fg.$description: audit-2026-05-13 — outline badge fg cascades to blue.700 (#1D4ED8) in dark which fails AA on transparent → body bg #18181B. Match subtle.fg for readability.
audit-2026-05-13 — match subtle.fg; was red.700 cascade which fails AA on body dark bg.
audit-2026-05-13 — match subtle.fg; was green.700 cascade which fails AA on body dark bg.

audit-2026-05-12 — force gray.900 LIGHT value. Semantic cascade resolves filled.fg = {color.gray.900} which inverts to #FAFAFA in dark, giving ~2:1 on amber.500 bg. Hardcoding the dark hex keeps the dark-text-on-yellow contrast (~12:1) consistent with the light theme rendering.
audit-2026-05-13 — match subtle.fg; was amber.700 cascade which fails AA on body dark bg.
audit-2026-05-13 — match subtle.fg; was cyan.700 cascade which fails AA on body dark bg.

alert.$description: Subtle backgrounds — same logic as badge. r3 (audit-2026-05-06): titles for ALL subtle / outline appearances pulled to plain white so they read 'distinct' rather than 'tinted-but-similar'. Subtle desc for danger/success/warning/info bumped one step lighter so body text is readable. Solid desc explicitly held at light X.100 instead of inverting to dark X.50 (which produced dark-on-bright text in dark mode). Solid neutral bg pulled to subtle-neutral level so it stops reading as a near-white card.

fg-title.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r3 — plain white, was #DBEAFE which read as same-toned as desc

fg-desc.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r4 — match solid.fg-desc; was #BFDBFE which read too dim against the darker bg

border.$description: audit-2026-05-06 — subtle alerts in dark feel cleaner without a border; the tinted bg defines the box

fg-desc.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r3 — hold at blue-100 light; without override the alias cascades to blue.50 dark = #172554 which is dark text on bright bg — wrong

bg.$description: audit-2026-05-06 — outline alerts were on surface-base (#18181B); dropped to page bg so the colored border carries identity

fg-title.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r5 — blue-200 light, brand-tinted title (was plain white #FAFAFA but user wants colored title for outline)

fg-desc.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r5 — gray-800 light, brighter than gray.700-inv #D4D4D8 which was barely visible

bg.$description: audit-2026-05-06 — same as outline; left-accent identity comes from the colored bar

fg-title.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r5 — blue-200 light, visible brand title

fg-desc.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r5 — gray-800 light, brighter body text

border.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r5 — surface-base level; border was gray.200-inv #27272A which felt too prominent

accent.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r5 — muted brand bar (was blue.600 dark = #2563EB, too saturated/bright for accent)

fg-desc.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r4 — match solid.fg-desc; red-100 light

accent.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r5 — muted dark red bar

fg-desc.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r4 — match solid.fg-desc; green-100 light

accent.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r5 — muted dark green bar

fg-desc.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r4 — amber-50 light, matches solid pattern

accent.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r5 — muted dark amber bar

fg-desc.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r4 — match solid.fg-desc; cyan-100 light

accent.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r5 — muted dark cyan bar

fg-title.$description: audit-2026-05-06 — near-white title on the gray-200 dark subtle bg

fg-desc.$description: audit-2026-05-06 — gray-400 light, readable secondary on gray-200 dark

bg.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r3 — match subtle.neutral; default cascade gave gray.900 inverted = #F4F4F5 (near-white card) which read as 'light bg' in dark mode

border.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r4 — match bg so the border disappears; other solid alerts naturally have border = bg, but neutral was using gray.900-inv = #F4F4F5 which left a bright outline on the new dark bg

fg-title.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r5 — explicit white title (cascade gave gray.900 inverted = #F4F4F5 which user reported as 'not visible' — likely a perception issue from the bordered card; explicit value to be safe)

border.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r5 — gray-200 dark, less bright than gray.300 inversion #3F3F46

accent.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r5 — gray-300 dark, muted neutral bar

primary.$description: Dark-mode primary button overrides. Includes: (1) designer-pass FG colors so text reads on saturated bg, (2) muted disabled bg/border so disabled blends with surface-base, (3) less saturated subtle bg / outline border / outline+ghost hover-active bg per audit feedback (2026-05-05).

disabled.$description: audit-2026-05-05 — disabled blends with surface-base, less 'светлая' feel

loading.$description: blue-500 dark — bright loading bg so spinner stays readable

disabled.$description: audit-2026-05-05 — muted disabled fg on the new darker disabled bg

loading.$description: light loader on saturated loading bg

rest.$description: audit-2026-05-05 — muted dark navy, less saturated than blue-50 inversion (#172554) which read as too colored
designer-pass

hover.$description: audit-2026-05-05 — match subtle.bg.rest, soft brand-tinted hover surface

rest.$description: audit-2026-05-12 — blue-300 light; was inheriting blue.600 dark (#2563EB) which gave 3.77:1 on surface-base (fails AA). Matches subtle.fg.rest.

hover.$description: audit-2026-05-12 — blue-200 light, lighter than rest for hover affordance on tinted bg #1E2A4F

active.$description: audit-2026-05-05 r2 — blue-200 light, readable on darker active bg

disabled.$description: audit-2026-05-05 r2 — much more muted disabled fg, not 'too bright' for a disabled state

rest.$description: audit-2026-05-05 — muted brand border, less saturated than blue.300 inversion (#1D4ED8) which felt too bright
audit-2026-05-12 — match primary.outline.fg.rest; was inheriting blue.600 dark (#2563EB) which fails AA on surface-base
audit-2026-05-05 — muted dark red, less saturated than red-50 inversion
designer-pass
audit-2026-05-12 — red-300 light; was inheriting red.600 dark (#DC2626) which gave ~4.0:1 on surface-base (fails AA). Matches subtle.fg.rest.

hover.$description: audit-2026-05-12 — red-200 light, lighter than rest for hover on tinted bg #3A1518

active.$description: audit-2026-05-05 r2 — red-200 light, readable on darker active bg

rest.$description: audit-2026-05-05 — muted dark red border
audit-2026-05-12 — match danger.outline.fg.rest; was inheriting red.600 dark (#DC2626) which fails AA on surface-base

default.$description: Neutral default in dark = elevated dark button (gray-300/400/500 dark). Was inheriting gray.900 inverted (#F4F4F5) which produced a near-white button with dark text — visually inconsistent in dark mode. Audit (2026-05-05) flipped to a medium-dark elevated surface with light text.

rest.$description: gray-300 dark — elevated above surface-base

loading.$description: lighter so the dark loader stays visible

rest.$description: audit-2026-05-05 — light text on medium-dark elevated button

disabled.$description: audit-2026-05-05 r3 — match primary/danger.default.fg.disabled for visual consistency across colors

rest.$description: audit-2026-05-05 — gray-200 dark, muted dark
designer-pass
audit-2026-05-05 r2 — gray-800 light, slightly brighter than gray.700 default

hover.$description: audit-2026-05-05 r2 — near white on hover bg #27272A for unmistakable contrast

rest.$description: audit-2026-05-05 — neutral outline border = gray-300 dark
audit-2026-05-05 r2 — match outline.fg.rest

border.$description: audit-2026-05-06 — match button outline-neutral border (gray.300 dark); was gray.200-inv (#27272A) which was too subtle to define the card outline against the page bg in dark

bg-elevated.$description: audit-2026-05-06 — slight lift above card.bg (#18181B) so elevated cards visually sit above default ones in dark; was the same hex which made elevated indistinguishable

avatar.$description: Dark-mode avatar chrome. Default bg/fg cascade (gray.200/gray.700) was OK but other appearances aliased X.100 / X.700 — the dark inversions (X.100 dark = saturated mid-darks like #1E3A8A, #14532D) read as 'too saturated / too light' against the dark page bg. Pulled bgs down to muted-dark variants matching the audit-tuned button subtle palette. neutral was the worst: bg.neutral aliased gray.900 → #F4F4F5 (near-white), now lifted to gray.300 dark.

default.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r2 — gray.200 dark; tried surface-base #18181B but felt too dark for an avatar; back to gray-200

neutral.$description: audit-2026-05-06 — gray-300 dark; was gray.900-inv (#F4F4F5) which left a near-white avatar in dark mode

primary.$description: audit-2026-05-06 — blue-300 light, readable on the new muted dark bg

neutral.$description: audit-2026-05-06 — light text on the new gray-300-dark neutral bg

ring.$description: audit-2026-05-06 — page bg; was color.white inverted = #18181B which left a visible 'lifted' ring around the avatar in dark mode. Page-matching ring is invisible against the page so the avatar looks like a clean disc.

bg.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r2 — gray.200 dark; same logic as avatar.bg.default — surface-base felt too dark

fg.$description: audit-2026-05-06 r2 — bright white; gray.700-inv (#D4D4D8) read 'too pale' for the +N number on a dark count chip

hover.$description: audit-2026-05-06 — gray.500 dark, brighter than input.border.hover (#52525B); the small checkbox box needs more contrast on hover

selected.$description: audit-2026-05-07 r2 — blue-100 light, one step brighter than the r1 #BFDBFE which was still hard to read on the dark selected bg

segmented.$description: Dark-mode SegmentedControl. Track inherited gray.100-inv = #18181B (= surface-base, blended with page bg). Bumped to gray.200-inv (#27272A) so the track outline reads, and selected segment lifted further (#3F3F46) so the selected affordance reads above the track.

selected.$description: audit-2026-05-06 — gray.300 dark; one step above track for clear elevation

input.$description: Dark-mode input chrome. bg.default was inheriting color.white (= surface-base = #18181B) which read as 'still light' against the page bg in dark; pulled to a deliberately darker value so the field surface is unmistakably dark in dark mode. r2 (2026-05-05): border / fg / placeholder values realigned with Button outline-neutral so input chrome reads with the same visual weight as that button on a dark surface.

default.$description: audit-2026-05-05 — distinctly darker than surface-base (#18181B); slight lift from page (#09090B) so the field is still defined

filled.$description: audit-2026-05-05 — filled variant gets a more visible lift (= surface-base)

disabled.$description: audit-2026-05-05 — disabled blends with page bg, recessed look

rest.$description: audit-2026-05-05 r3 — gray-300 dark; on the deeper input bg #0E0F13 this lands at the same perceived weight as button outline-neutral border on transparent bg

hover.$description: audit-2026-05-05 r3 — gray-400 dark, one step up for clear hover affordance

disabled.$description: audit-2026-05-05 r3 — gray-200 dark; barely-there but visible enough that the disabled field still reads as a field

default.$description: audit-2026-05-05 r2 — gray-800 light, matches button outline-neutral rest fg; was #71717A which read 'too pale' for typed input value

disabled.$description: audit-2026-05-05 r2 — match button outline disabled fg, low-prominence disabled label

default.$description: audit-2026-05-12 — bumped from #52525B to zinc-400. The deeper input bg (#0E0F13) gave only 2.4:1 against the old #52525B; #A1A1AA gives ~6.5:1, meeting AA without making the placeholder look like real input text.

bg.$description: audit-2026-05-06 — slight lift above surface-base (#18181B); was inheriting white-inv = #18181B which read indistinguishable from the page bg layered behind it

border.$description: audit-2026-05-06 — gray.300 dark, matches the popover trigger surfaces (input / button outline / card / dropdown-menu* — *menu uses gray.200 still)

rest.$description: designer-pass

selected.$description: Near-white for max readability on dark page bg. Saturated blue-600 from the light theme has poor contrast against #09090b page bg; underline still carries the brand-blue accent.
Match selected fg for consistent dark-mode tab read.

nav-item.$description: Dark-mode active nav-item fg/icon — light brand tints so the active state is unmistakable on the dark sidebar bg.

active.$description: audit-2026-05-07 — blue-100 light; was blue.700-inv = #1D4ED8 (same blue tone as the active bg #172554)
audit-2026-05-07 — blue-200 light; one step less prominent than the label so the icon doesn't dominate

header.$description: Dark-mode header nav-item active fg. Without this override, header.nav-item.fg.active cascades to blue.700 light (#1D4ED8), which on the dark header bg #18181B gives ~1.4:1 — fails AA hard.

active.$description: audit-2026-05-12 — blue-400 light, ~7:1 on dark header bg. Mirrors accent.primary.fg dark override.

rest.$description: designer-pass

text.$description: Dark-theme overrides for the text family. `strong` and `default` cascade through primitive inversion automatically; `muted` and `disabled` need explicit lifts so they keep AA contrast and stay visually distinct from each other on the dark page bg.

muted.$description: gray-600 in dark = #A1A1AA — passes AA on gray-900 dark page bg (=#18181B)

disabled.$description: audit-2026-05-12 — bumped from gray.500 (=#71717A in dark, 3.46:1 fails AA) to gray.600 (=#A1A1AA, 6.5:1). Visually collapses with `muted` in dark; the disabled affordance now comes from `cursor: not-allowed` + reduced font-weight rather than dimmer fg.

optional.$description: audit-2026-05-13 — gray.500 cascade gives #71717A in dark, 3.46:1 on #18181B. gray.600 dark = #A1A1AA, 6.5:1.

tree.$description: Dark-theme tree node overrides. Selected fg+bg flip to muted-dark navy / readable accent (was blue.700 on blue.50 = 1.4:1 in dark). Disabled fg uses text-muted equivalent.

selected.$description: blue-300 light, readable on the dark navy selected bg

disabled.$description: matches the muted/disabled collapse in dark

selected.$description: muted dark navy, matches button primary.subtle.bg.rest

surface.$description: Dark-theme overrides for the surface family. `base` (= white) and `subtle` (= gray.50) cascade correctly through primitive inversion; `muted` and `strong` need explicit lifts so hover / pressed states stay visually distinct from `base` (gray-100 inverts to the same hex as gray-900, which would collapse the elevation difference).

muted.$description: gray-200 dark = #27272A — sits one step lighter than `base` (=#18181B)

strong.$description: gray-300 dark = #3F3F46 — pressed / active row distinguishable from `muted`

accent.$description: Dark-theme accent fg overrides — readable brand shades on dark page bg, decoupled from action-shade primitive inversion
